task_scenario: bounded knowledge tasks and source verification

intervention.purpose: bounded scaffolded support through scripted scenes

next_action: ask AI to regenerate with supporting evidence
flag and do not use this output

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant tries to extract a reusable rule rather than only solve the current item.
examples: asks what rule should be applied next time

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant explicitly anchors decisions in the facts package or source-text mismatch.
examples: checks whether claim strength matches evidence strength

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant compares alternatives by boundary language and evidence support rather than style.
examples: prefers cautious answer because it matches evidence limits

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant notices uncertainty, requests verification, or withholds acceptance pending more evidence.
examples: states that the answer cannot be trusted yet
chooses verify or inspect before use

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant treats the AI answer as authoritative without sufficiently checking evidence.
examples: defers to the AI answer despite mismatch signals

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant focuses on wording polish while missing the underlying evidence problem.
examples: rewrites style without fixing unsupported claim
treats fabricated citation as a phrasing issue

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant notices that the AI claim exceeds the original study's scope, sample, or context.
examples: notes that the study only covered one institution or discipline

items.writing_goal_zh: I am drafting background context for a report on youth job-search services and want to add one sentence: "Some studies suggest that when applicants use generative tools to rewrite resumes, one of their main concerns is that the result becomes too templated." My prompt to the AI is: "Based on this sentence, give me one citation I can place at the end."
items.ai_answer: You could cite: Lin, Y., Romero, J., & Singh, P. (2023). Interview Study of Young Job Seekers Using AI Resume Tools. China Digital Society Review, 12(4), 55-73.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Database search: no record was found for China Digital Society Review, Vol. 12, Issue 4, pp. 55-73.
Database search: no DOI or stable link was found for this item.
Closest result: Lin, Y. & Singh, P. (2022). Interview Notes on Digital Job-Search Support in Employment Service Centers. working paper.
That working paper is about digital job-search support in service centers, not generative AI resume rewriting tools.
The authors partly overlap, but the study object, title framing, and publication format do not match the AI citation.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this AI-suggested citation?

next_action: flag and do not use this output
gold_label.reason_anchor: The key citation details cannot be validated, and the closest real source only overlaps on authorship rather than supporting the title, publication format, and study object given by the AI.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a historical overview of how local gazetteers are used. My prompt is: "Tax records in local gazetteers can help researchers trace changes in regional commercial activity in the late Qing."
items.ai_answer: You can use this source: Wang, H. & Keller, M. (2024). Merchant Tax Records in Late Qing Local Gazetteers. Historical Text Studies, 18(2), 88-109.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Database lookup: a real 2024 source by Wang and Keller with the same title is found.
The actual source is an edited-volume chapter, not a journal article in Historical Text Studies.
The real source does not have journal-style volume or issue metadata.
The abstract indicates that merchant tax records in local gazetteers can be used to analyze changes in commercial activity in specific late-Qing regions.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Would you accept, revise, or reject this AI suggestion? Why?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The source appears real and topically relevant, but the publication form and journal metadata supplied by the AI are inaccurate, so the citation cannot be copied directly.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am drafting two lines of investor communication from a Q3 earnings-call summary. My prompt to the AI is: "Summarize the business impact after the AI customer-service rollout in a sell-side-note tone."
items.ai_answer: Management commentary suggests that after the AI customer-service rollout, service efficiency improved, the margin rebound indicates that the initiative is already feeding into financial performance, and customer retention appears to have remained stable in the short run.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Source title: Q3 Earnings Call Summary.
Management states that after the AI customer-service tool went live, customer-service labor cost and average response time both declined in the quarter.
The summary also notes that operating margin improved in the same quarter, but it also mentions product-mix adjustment and lower marketing spend.
The material provides no evidence that customer retention stayed stable, and it does not show that the margin improvement was primarily caused by the AI tool.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this AI-generated analytical statement?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The source supports improved service efficiency and a margin rebound in the same quarter, but it does not support direct causal attribution to the AI rollout, nor does it support the added claim that customer retention remained stable.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a short note on data-processing procedures. My prompt is: "Before merging tables from multiple sources, date formats should be standardized and duplicate records should be checked."
items.ai_answer: Before merging tables from multiple sources, date formats should be standardized and duplicate records should be checked. The data-cleaning guide explicitly recommends these as basic steps.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Source title: Practical Guide to Merging Administrative Tables.
The guide explicitly recommends standardizing date and time formats before a merge.
The guide explicitly recommends checking duplicates and conflicting IDs before integration.
The source is procedural guidance rather than an outcome study, but these steps are directly recommended.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this AI-generated methodological suggestion?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The sentence accurately summarizes the guide’s direct recommendation and does not misstate procedural guidance as empirical performance evidence.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a historical overview of late-Qing newspaper circulation. My prompt is: "Please summarize research on the relationship between late-Qing newspaper circulation and urban public discussion."

ai_answers.A: Research has established that the expansion of newspapers in the late Qing steadily drove the formation of public discussion nationwide.
ai_answers.B: Some regional studies suggest that newspaper circulation may have expanded spaces for public discussion in specific cities, but there were still substantial differences across regions and periods.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Existing studies mainly analyze newspapers and public discussion in a few cities such as Shanghai and Tianjin.
These studies suggest that circulation may have expanded discussion spaces in those specific urban settings.
The authors caution that current evidence is insufficient for a nationwide conclusion and does not imply uniform effects across regions or periods.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Which answer is more trustworthy? How would you proceed?

gold_label.reason_anchor: Answer B preserves the regional evidence and its limits, whereas Answer A turns a small set of city-based studies into a national, stable conclusion.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a comment on a company’s latest earnings report. My prompt is: "Please summarize what this company’s latest report implies for margins."

ai_answers.A: The report shows that the company faced margin pressure this quarter, and management also signaled that short-term cost control remains important.
ai_answers.B: The report may reflect some noteworthy changes, but more observation is still needed.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: The Q2 report shows gross margin declining from 34% to 29%.
Management attributes the pressure to inventory adjustments and states that cost control remains a priority next quarter.
The material does not support a long-term trend claim, but it does support describing margin pressure in the current quarter.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Which answer is more trustworthy? Why?

gold_label.reason_anchor: Answer A is specific and aligned with the material, whereas Answer B is more vague and fails to capture the key point that the report clearly supports.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am preparing one user-insight line for a weekly product meeting. My prompt to the AI is: "Based on these interview notes, write the single most homepage-worthy finding."
items.ai_answer: Interviewees broadly treated “not understanding how the model made its recommendation” as the core barrier to using robo-advisory tools, which suggests that algorithm transparency remains the most sensitive issue for ordinary investors in this category.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: The study is based on interviews with 24 first-time robo-advisory users at two Shanghai branch locations.
A number of interviewees said they found it hard to understand why the system produced a given recommendation.
The authors also recorded other concerns among novice users, including unclear risk disclosures and switching costs.
The authors caution that the sample is limited to novice users in one city and should not be treated as representative of ordinary investors overall.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Would you keep, revise, or reject this AI summary?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The material supports at most that algorithm transparency was a common concern in this Shanghai novice-user interview set; it does not support elevating that concern into the core barrier for ordinary investors overall.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am summarizing the results of a city-traffic data-cleaning experiment. My prompt is: "Please summarize the effectiveness of this cleaning rule set."
items.ai_answer: This cleaning rule set has been proven to identify anomalous GPS records reliably in urban traffic data nationwide.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: The experiment is based on one month of taxi GPS data from three districts in Hangzhou.
On that dataset, the rule set improved anomaly detection.
The authors caution that there is no evidence yet for direct transfer to other cities, seasons, or sensing systems.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this AI summary?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The study supports effectiveness on a specific Hangzhou dataset, not the broader claim that the rules work reliably across urban traffic data nationwide.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing an industry note. My prompt is: "Independent research reports indicate that mid-sized retail banks are using generative AI to reduce customer-service costs."
items.ai_answer: You can cite: Garcia, L., Moore, T., & Zhou, X. (2024). Generative AI Reduces Service Costs Across Mid-Sized Retail Banks. Journal of Applied Financial Strategy, 7(3), 41-59.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Database lookup: no record is found for Journal of Applied Financial Strategy, 7(3), 41-59.
Database lookup: no DOI is resolved for the citation above.
Related search hit: Garcia, L. & Zhou, X. (2023). AI Pilots in Retail Banking Service Operations. Industry webinar brief.
That brief discusses pilots in two banks, not a formal study across mid-sized retail banks.
The author names partially overlap, but the title, publication form, and study scope do not match the AI citation.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this AI citation suggestion?

next_action: flag and do not use this output
gold_label.reason_anchor: The citation cannot be verified, and the closest related source does not support the journal, pagination, or scope written by the AI.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a short note on data-engineering practice. My prompt is: "Manual review after stratified sampling can help reveal systematic bias in auto-labeled data."
items.ai_answer: You can cite: Ibrahim, D. & Chen, P. (2024). Stratified Audit Checks for Auto-Labeled Corpora. Data Governance Studies, 6(1), 13-29.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Database lookup: a real 2024 source by Ibrahim and Chen with a closely matching title is found.
The actual source is a workshop proceedings paper, not a journal article in Data Governance Studies.
The real source does not have journal-style volume or issue metadata.
The abstract indicates that stratified manual audits did help reveal systematic bias in auto-labeled corpora.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Can this AI suggestion be used directly? Why or why not?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The source is relevant and likely real, but the publication form and journal metadata given by the AI are inaccurate, so the citation should not be copied directly.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a short product-research conclusion. My prompt is: "Interviews can help teams identify recurring pain points more quickly, but further validation is still needed before formal decisions are made."
items.ai_answer: Existing research suggests that interviews can help teams identify recurring pain points more quickly, but further validation is still needed before formal decisions are made.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Source title: Early-Stage User Interviews in Product Discovery.
The teams used interviews to surface repeated user pain points more quickly.
The authors caution that interview material is exploratory and should be followed by broader validation before major decisions.
The source does not claim that interview findings alone are market-wide evidence.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Would you keep, revise, or reject this statement?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The AI sentence preserves both the supported benefit and the supported limitation, without turning exploratory interviews into strong stand-alone decision evidence.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing one sentence in a historiographical methods review and want to summarize the value of genealogical records. My prompt to the AI is: "Based on this methods paper, write one sentence that could go directly into the paragraph."
items.ai_answer: The article argues that although genealogical records are biased, they can still serve as a fairly reliable basis for reconstructing the everyday economic behavior of ordinary households.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Source title: Using Genealogies as Historical Clues.
The paper argues that genealogical records can provide clues about family strategy, marriage arrangement, and some economic activities.
The author repeatedly discusses bias, silences, class skew, and representativeness limits in genealogical materials.
The paper does not claim that genealogical records can fairly reliably reconstruct the full everyday economic behavior of ordinary households.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this statement?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The source supports “providing clues,” not “fairly reliable reconstruction of everyday economic behavior”; the AI turns a methodological clue into a much stronger reconstruction claim.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a short review of data-quality management. My prompt is: "Please summarize the role of visual dashboards in anomaly detection."

ai_answers.A: Visual dashboards can help analysts notice anomalous patterns more quickly, but raw records still need to be inspected before causes are identified.
ai_answers.B: Visual dashboards can reliably detect and explain all data-quality problems because they provide a complete view of anomalies.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: Dashboards helped analysts notice unusual spikes and trends more quickly.
Analysts still needed raw records to determine the causes of anomalies.
Not all data-quality issues were directly visible in the dashboard view.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Which answer is more trustworthy? What would you do next?

gold_label.reason_anchor: Answer A keeps both the practical value and the boundary of dashboards, whereas Answer B turns a useful monitoring tool into an all-purpose explanation engine.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing one sentence in a market-research methods review. My prompt to the AI is: "Based on this article, write one sentence describing what AI summaries are useful for when working with interview records."

ai_answers.A: AI summaries can clearly speed up early-stage organization, and many teams will directly use them to derive interview conclusions.
ai_answers.B: AI summaries are suitable for early-stage synthesis, but before writing formal conclusions, researchers still need to return to the original interview records to check context and limitations.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: After using AI summaries, the research team completed the first-pass organization of interview notes more quickly.
Some AI summaries omitted qualifiers or blended statements from different interviewees.
The authors recommend revisiting the original interview records before turning the material into formal conclusions.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Which answer is more trustworthy? Why?

gold_label.reason_anchor: B preserves the efficiency value of AI summaries while retaining the required validation step of checking original records; A turns a common but risky shortcut into an endorsed practice.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing a history-and-humanities overview. My prompt is: "Please summarize what local archives show about regional tax practices."
items.ai_answer: Archival studies show that tax enforcement across all late-Qing regions depended heavily on local negotiation.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: The study examines local archives from two counties in the Jiangnan region.
In those two cases, tax enforcement did indeed rely heavily on local negotiation.
The authors caution that there is no evidence yet for generalizing this pattern to late-Qing China as a whole.

items.participant_prompt_zh: Would you accept, revise, or reject this statement?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The study and the AI sentence concern the same kind of outcome, but the evidence covers only two Jiangnan counties. The AI improperly extends that finding to all late-Qing regions.

items.writing_goal_zh: I am writing one line for a retail-industry comment. My prompt to the AI is: "Based on this case material, use one sentence to summarize how generative AI affects profit margin and close the paragraph."
items.ai_answer: Based on the current case, generative AI is already showing margin-improvement effects in retail, especially in customer service and product-copy workflows, and that improvement will likely keep spreading across most sub-sectors.

evidence_card.title: Facts
bullets: The source material describes a three-month generative-AI pilot at one apparel retailer.
The pilot was mainly used for customer-service reply drafting and product-copy generation, and labor time declined in both workflows.
Margin improved slightly in the same period, but the material also notes lower inventory discounting and seasonal sales recovery.
The author cautions that this is a single-company, short-horizon case and should not be generalized to other retail sub-sectors or treated as evidence of a lasting margin effect.

items.participant_prompt_zh: How would you handle this statement?

gold_label.reason_anchor: The material supports at most that process efficiency improved and margin also improved slightly in one apparel-retail case; it does not support treating that pattern as a durable rule for most retail sub-sectors.

hallucination-weak.learning_objective_zh: Learn to check existence, metadata match, and topical fit before using an AI citation.
script_lines: A citation can look academic and still be unusable.
Before trusting it, check three things: existence, metadata match, and topical fit.
If the source cannot be verified, do not soften it. Reject it.
In your earlier response to {failed_item_id}, the key problem was: {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: if the citation itself cannot be validated, rewrite instead of patching.
hallucination-weak.learner_task_zh: Write one rule you would check before accepting an AI citation next time.
hallucination-weak.expected_signal: mentions existence or metadata match before use

script_lines: This citation looks polished, but what exactly makes it unreliable?
Point to one field or one mismatch that makes you stop trusting it.
Good. Now convert that observation into a reusable rule.
Complete this sentence: If a citation cannot be verified by metadata, I should ______.
hallucination-weak.learner_task_zh: Identify the most suspicious field or mismatch and turn it into a reusable rule.
hallucination-weak.expected_signal: reject or rewrite rather than continue using the citation

verification-weak.learning_objective_zh: Learn to distinguish between a source existing and a source actually supporting the claim.
script_lines: A real source can still be used incorrectly.
Your job is not only to check whether a paper exists, but whether it supports this exact wording.
When the evidence is weaker than the claim, revise the claim.
In {failed_item_id}, the mismatch was: {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: align claim strength with source strength.
verification-weak.learner_task_zh: Rewrite the original sentence to a weaker version that aligns with the evidence.
verification-weak.expected_signal: shifts from strong causal wording to limited or suggestive wording

script_lines: Which exact phrase in the AI answer goes beyond the source?
Is the problem causality, certainty, or outcome inflation?
Now write one rule for future reading: when should you weaken a claim instead of accepting it?
verification-weak.learner_task_zh: Identify the overstated phrase and write a rule about when to weaken a claim.
verification-weak.expected_signal: points to unsupported causal or outcome language

trust-calibration-weak.learning_objective_zh: Learn to compare two AI answers by trustworthiness, not fluency.
script_lines: The more fluent answer is not always the more trustworthy one.
A more trustworthy answer usually shows evidence boundaries.
Look for cautious wording, explicit limits, and alignment with the source.
In {failed_item_id}, the stronger answer was less trustworthy because {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: trust the answer that stays closer to what the evidence can support.
trust-calibration-weak.learner_task_zh: Write one signal that makes an AI answer sound confident but actually lack evidence.
trust-calibration-weak.expected_signal: mentions overconfident wording, missing evidence, universal claims, or causal inflation

script_lines: If you had to defend your choice in front of a reviewer, what evidence-based reason would you give?
Do not say it sounds better. Say what makes it more trustworthy.
Complete this rule: When two AI answers disagree, I should prefer the one that ______.
trust-calibration-weak.learner_task_zh: Defend your choice with evidence-based reasoning and write it as a rule.
trust-calibration-weak.expected_signal: refers to evidence anchoring, boundary language, or explicit uncertainty

scope-generalization-weak.learning_objective_zh: Learn to check whether the AI overgeneralized limited research findings to a broader scope.
script_lines: An AI answer can make a finding sound universal when the study was actually quite limited.
Before accepting a broad claim, check: What was the sample? What was the context? What were the authors' own stated limitations?
If the original study was small, narrow, or discipline-specific, the claim should reflect that scope.
In your earlier response to {failed_item_id}, the key problem was: {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: match the scope of the claim to the scope of the evidence.
scope-generalization-weak.learner_task_zh: Write a rule you would use to check if an AI claim exceeds the study's scope.
scope-generalization-weak.expected_signal: mentions checking sample size, study context, or author-stated limitations before accepting broad claims

script_lines: This claim sounds broad. Can you find the specific words that go beyond the original study?
Point to one word or phrase that makes the claim wider than what the evidence supports.
Good. Now convert that into a reusable rule.
Complete this sentence: When an AI claim uses words like 'all' or 'universally', I should first check ______.
scope-generalization-weak.learner_task_zh: Identify the most overgeneralized word and turn it into a reusable rule.
scope-generalization-weak.expected_signal: mentions checking whether the source's sample, context, or conclusions actually support that level of generalization
